title: VirION: Long-read, low-input, viral metagenomic sequencing with MinION
author: Joanna JWD Warwick-Dugdale
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p8fdrtn

steps.instruction: Ensure DNA is not stuck to tube by gently flicking; then spin down; transfer <1 μg DNA in 47 μl NFW/EB into the top compartment of a Covaris g-TUBE; screw on lid

Shear DNA: Spin g-TUBE for 1 min at 6000 rpm (check all DNA has transferred into lower compartment of g-TUBE; if not, repeat 1 min spin); flip tube over (so base points upwards) and spin again for 1 min at 6000 rpm (check all DNA has transferred into lid of g-TUBE; if not, repeat 1 min spin); Keep processing time <15 min; *if a microcentrifuge other than model specified is used, spin speed may have to be optimised to obtain 8 Kbp fragments

keep g-TUBE base upward, carefully unscrew tube and transfer DNA from lid to a fresh LoBind Eppendorf or (if proceeding directly to stage ‘B’) 0.2 ml PCR tube Retain 1 μl of sheared DNA for later quantification via QuBit fluorimeter Assessment of fragment size Bioanalyzer/TapeStation not usually possible due to dilute nature of DNA
To 45 μl of fragmented and repaired DNA, add: 5 μl NFW 7 μl Ultra II End-Prep buffer 3 μl Ultra II End-Prep enzyme mix (resulting in 60 μl total reaction volume)
Mix by gentle flicking, then spin down (transfer end-prep reaction to a 0.2 ml PCR tube, if needed)
Using thermocycler program 1, incubate for: 5 mins at 20 °C 5 min at 65 °C, 1 min 20 °C Then transfer to fresh DNA LoBind tube
Resuspend AMPure beads (vortex); add 60 μl of beads to end-prep reaction; mix by gentle flicking
Incubate for 5 mins at RT, either on rotator, or gently flick every ~1 min Undetermined, Room temp.
Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~2 min)
Leaving the tube on the magnet, carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant
Keep tube on magnet, and wash beads with 200 µl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol (without disturbing the pellet; leave ~15 secs)
Remove the 80% ethanol using a pipette and discard
Repeat wash (steps 11 and 12)

Spin down briefly, replace on magnet, pipette off residual wash; briefly allow to dry (~1-2 min) It is important beads are allowed to dry to ensure all ethanol has been removed, however, over drying will result in some of the DNA not eluting efficiently leading to reduced recovery
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellet in 31 µl nuclease-free water by gentle flicking
Incubate for 2 minutes at 55 °C (heat block) Undetermined, In heat block
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~2 min)
Transfer eluate to fresh DNA LoBind tube; Retain 1 µl of fragmented and end-prepped DNA for later quantification (QuBit fluorimeter) Recovery aim: ≥ 20 ng (dependent on starting quantity).

To 30 μl of end-prepped DNA, add: 20 μl of PCA 50 μl Blunt/TA Ligase Master Mix (resulting in 100 μl total reaction volume) Mix by gentle flicking, then spin down Note: mix between each addition by gentle flicking, and spin down
Incubate at RT for 10 minutes Undetermined, Room temp.
Resuspend AMPure beads (vortex); add 40 μl of beads to PCR adapter reaction; mix by gentle flicking This 0.4x beadwash removes small fragments of template DNA before PCR step
Incubate for 5 mins at RT, either on rotator, or gently flick every ~1 min Undetermined, Room temp.
Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~3 min)
Leaving the tube on the magnet, carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant
Keep tube on magnet, and wash beads with 200 µl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol (without disturbing the pellet; leave ~15 secs)
Remove the 80% ethanol using a pipette and discard
repeat wash (steps 25 and 26)
Briefly spin down, replace on magnet, pipette of residual wash; briefly allow to dry (note importance of drying time, as above)
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellet in 26 µl nuclease-free water by gentle flicking
Incubate for 2 minutes at 55 °C (heat block) Undetermined, Heat block
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~3 min)
Transfer eluate to fresh DNA LoBind tube Conduct a High Sensitivity (HS) QuBit fluorimeter quantification on 1 µl of PCR adapted and ligated DNA (also quantify retained DNA from stages ‘A’ and ‘B’)

Calculate volume required to add ~20 ng of PCR-adapter ligated DNA into the PCR reaction below (e.g. 2.7 ng/µl x 8 = 21.6 ng; use 8 µl) If not proceeding directly to stage ‘D’, PCR-adapter ligated DNA should be stored post clean-up at -20°C.

Set up the following PCR reactions (total volume 100 µl) in 0.2 ml thin-walled tubes, on ice: *40 µl NFW 2 µl PRM (primers) *8 µl PCR adapted and ligated DNA (from stage ‘C’) 50 µl NEB Next High Fidelity 2X master mix :Mix by gentle flicking and spin down briefly *Adjust volume of NFW and DNA as necessary for a 100µl reaction with ~20 ng of PCR-adapter ligated DNA, using the volumes of PRM and NEB Next master mix specified Set up as many reactions as possible to increase yield of amplicons for sequencing; also set up the PCR-adapter ligated lambda as a positive control

Quickly transfer PCR reactions to the thermal cycler and run the following (Program 2): Initial denaturation: 3 mins at 95°C; 15 cycles of: Denaturation: 15 secs at 95°C; Annealing: 15 at 62°C; Extension: 5 min at 72°C; Final Extension: 5 min at 72°C; Hold at 4°C
Spin down and transfer each reaction to a fresh DNA LoBind tube
Resuspend AMPure beads (vortex); add 40 μl of beads to each PCR reaction; mix by gentle flicking This 0.4x beadwash removes small amplification products
Incubate for 5 mins at RT, either on rotator, or gently flick every ~1 min Undetermined, Room temp.
Spin down the samples and pellet on a magnet until the eluates are clear and colourless (~3 min)
Leaving the tubes on the magnet, carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant
Keep tubes on magnet, and wash beads with 200 µl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol (without disturbing the pellets; leave ~15 secs)
Remove the 80% ethanol using a pipette and discard
Repeat wash (steps 41 & 42)
Briefly spin down, replace tubes on magnet, pipette of residual wash; briefly allow to dry (note importance of drying time, as above)
Remove the tubes from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellets in 26 µl nuclease-free water by gentle flicking
Incubate for 2 minutes at 55°C (heat block) Undetermined, Heat block
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~3 min)

Transfer eluate to fresh DNA LoBind tube Conduct Broad Range (BR) QuBit fluorimeter quantification on 1 µl of each PCR product Conduct Bionalyzer/Tapestation assay on 1 µl of each PCR product Check no high molecular weight product present in adapted and ligated PCR product via Tapestation/Bioanalyzer; very high MW product is associated with poor sequencing; if observed, repeat PCR with fewer cycles. If not proceeding directly to stage ‘E’, PCR products should be stored post clean-up at -20°C.
Concentrate (e.g. via SpeedVac) 3-3.5 µg of PCR product into 45 µl of NFW
To 45 µl of PCR product in NFW add: 5 μl NFW 7 μl Ultra II End-Prep buffer 3 μl Ultra II End-Prep enzyme mix (resulting in 60 μl total reaction volume)
Mix by gentle flicking, then spin down; transfer end-prep reaction to a 0.2 ml PCR tube
Using thermocycler program 1, incubate for: 5 mins at 20 °C 5 min at 65 °C, 1 min 20 °C Then transfer to fresh DNA LoBind tube
Resuspend AMPure beads (vortex); add 60 μl of beads to end-prep reaction; mix by gentle flicking
Incubate for 5 mins at RT, either on rotator, or gently flick every ~1 min Undetermined, Room temp.
Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~2 min)
Leaving the tube on the magnet, carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant
Keep tube on magnet, and wash beads with 200 µl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol (without disturbing the pellet; leave ~15 secs)
Remove the 80% ethanol using a pipette and discard
Repeat wash (steps 57 & 58)
Spin down, replace on magnet, pipette of residual wash; briefly allow to dry (note importance of drying time, as above)
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellet in 31 µl nuclease-free water by gentle flicking
Incubate for 2 minutes at 55 °C (heat block) Undetermined, Heat block
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~2 min)
Transfer eluate to fresh DNA LoBind tube Retain 1 µl of fragmented and repaired DNA for (later) quantification using a QuBit fluorimeter; Recovery aim: >2500 ng.

To 30 μl of end-prepped amplified DNA, add: 20 μl AMX 50 μl NEB Blunt / TA Master Mix (resulting in a final volume of 100 μl) Mix by gentle flicking, then spin down Mix between each addition by gentle flicking; spin down
Incubate at RT for 10 minutes Undetermined, Room temp.
Resuspend AMPure beads (vortex); add 40 μl of beads to the adapter ligation reaction; mix by gentle flicking
Incubate for 5 mins at RT, either on rotator, or gently flick every ~1 min Undetermined, Room temp.
Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~3 min)
Leaving the tube on the magnet, carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant
Add 140 μl of ABB to the beads; remove from magnet and resuspend by gentle flicking
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~5 min)
Leaving the tube on the magnet, very carefully remove supernatant using a pipette and discard
Repeat addition and removal of 140 μl of ABB (steps 71 -73)
Resuspend pelleted beads in 15 μl ELB by gentle but tenacious flicking; incubate at RT for 10 minutes Undetermined, Room temp.
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless (~5 min)

Remove and retain 15 µl of eluate into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube Place the tube of (adapted and tethered) library on ice until required for library loading This elution will form the basis of the library loading mix

Conduct a second elution on the beads: add 15 μl ELB to the pellet; resuspend; incubate at RT for 10 min; pellet on magnet; remove and retain second eluate into clean 1.5. ml tube; place this on ice If the first elution is somewhat low on DNA, this second one may form the basis of a second library loading mix. It may be worth loading the second library mix onto a flow cell at a point in the run where there does not appear to be enough DNA for the 'single pores' still available.

Quantify 1 µl of DNA library via QuBit fluorimeter (both elutions); also quantify DNA retained from previous stages at this time. Recovery aim for RT eluate: >1000 ng (~75ng/µl) ; ; Remove flow cell from 4°C to RT 30 min before performing QC and loading library (check for bubbles on ‘chip’; if bubbles noted, use alternative flow cell)
On host computer ensure that: The internet is working; >150 GB of memory is free on the C drive ; All sleep modes (including screensavers and log-offs) are disabled

Connect MinION to host computer (light and fan operation indicates that the device is successfully plugged in); open MinKNOW software; Check for MinKNOW software updates Check that the MinION configuration has been run prior to loading flow cell

Insert flow cell into MinION (orientated with flow chip adjacent to hinge); close cover; check that MinKNOW is reporting a ‘Flowcell connection’ number and flowcell ID (if not, close MinKNOW, eject MinION, and repeat)
Select running parameters: Label Experiment’ and ‘Sample ID’ (as required) Select the appropriate operation (i.e. Platform QC)

'Start' QC; to assist MinION gain correct temperature (approximately 34 °C), place MinION on insulating or conductive surface and modify airflow (as needed) Allow script to run to completion (this will QC the flow cell; total time ~10 min); note number of pore channels available (if <1000, consider using a different flow cell) Proceed with preparation of priming buffer
Mix RBF thoroughly by vortexing/pipetting; briefly spin down; prepare priming buffer by adding 576 μl of RBF to 624 μl NFW (total: 1200); mix by vortexing, spin down.
Open the Minion lid and flow cell ‘Priming Port’ (turn clockwise)

To ensure liquid-liquid contact between priming buffer flow cell buffer, and a continuous buffer flow from the Priming Port port across the sensor array: Place pipette tip of a Gilson 1000 μl pipette at flow cell Priming Port; hold vertically and slowly wind pipette (anticlockwise) to draw back a small volume of the (yellow) QC buffer from the flow cell (a few µls) Visually check that there is continuous buffer from the Priming Port across the sensor array; continue to wind pipette if necessary to remove any small bubbles, but do not draw bubbles from post-sensor channel into the array

Avoiding the introduction of air bubbles, load 800 µl of the priming mix via the Priming Port: wind a vertical Gilson pipette (clockwise), use pipette plunger (with care) under 200-100 µl volume, and remove pipette with a few µls left in tip

Proceed with preparing library for loading while flow cells ‘primes’ for 5 min Secondary priming of flow cell with 200 μl priming buffer (see below) should take place immediately before loading the library

To a fresh low bind tube, add the following (in order): 35 μl RBF; 2.5 μl NFW; 25.5 μl LLB; 12 μl of adapted and tethered DNA library (i.e.: ‘RT eluate’) (Total library loading mix: 75 μl) Mix by gentle flicking (gently spin down if needed) LLB will settle out very quickly; resupend by pipetting prior to transfter

Open the Spot-ON sample port cover with care Very carefully and slowly, add 200 μl of priming buffer to the flow cell via the Priming Port (NOT the Spot-ON Sample Port), avoiding bubbles and maintaining liquid-liquid contact as before (including removal of a few μls beforehand if needed); proceed immediately to next step
Resuspend beads in the library loading mix via pipette, and immediately load into the Spot-ON Sample Port in a dropwise fashion ; ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next
Gently replace the Spot-ON Sample Port cover ensuring that the bung enters the port; close the Priming Port cover and replace the MinION lid
Wait for 1 hour prior to starting sequencing run

Complete experiment parameters: Choose correct library prep. kit (SQK-LSK108) Select correct flow cell (FLO-MIN106; R9.4) Switch base calling to 'Off' (select other parameters as required; 48 hour run recommended)

Click on 'Begin Experiment' and: Note new number of pores reported in pre-sequencing QC ('Mux') Check pore occupancy figures (i.e. high ‘in-stand pore’ to ‘single pore’ ratio) Monitor messages in the Message panel in the MinKNOW GUI Allow the protocol to proceed in MinKNOW until it reports that the run is complete, or use ‘Stop’ in MinKNOW control panel to finish protocol The read files are stored in :\data\reads (? ‘C\MinION_data\reads) Base call reads after sequencing has been completed.
